claim: ECC's loop family is best treated as a pattern taxonomy plus production-stack sketch, not as a ready-to-run portable runtime.
gaps: Need upstream raw snapshot and license/attribution inspection if implementation imports text.

claim: Similar loop skills converge on bounded iteration, file-backed state, measurable acceptance or evaluator signals, quality gates, and explicit stop/recovery rules.
gaps: Some sources are skill registries rather than upstream raw files.

claim: Provider support is asymmetric: Codex, Claude, and Copilot expose native skills and agent/subagent surfaces, while DeepSeek is primarily a model/API backend with CodeWhale as the relevant third-party coding harness in this repo.
gaps: DeepSeek/CodeWhale runtime behavior should be probed locally before claiming active support.

claim: This repo already has the integration spine needed for autonomous research loops: research briefing, deep-research ledgers, agent-group discussion, external CLI delegation policy, runtime wrappers, target adapters, and verification gates.
gaps: Needs a panel-approved implementation plan before code changes.

claim: Autonomous research loops in this repo should remain parent-owned and evidence-gated; external provider outputs should enter as validated artifacts or evidence, not self-authorizing conclusions.
gaps: Cross-provider live dispatch still needs explicit confirmation and runtime probes.

claim: The v1 skill should be named autonomous-research-loop, with autonomous-agent-loop and continuous-agent-loop retained only as aliases or discovery text.
gaps: Maintainer may choose a different public name, but panel consensus favored the narrower research scope.

claim: Any runtime companion for v1 should be offline-only and must not dispatch provider CLIs, spawn agents, use the network, mutate the repository, or write outside the declared run directory.
gaps: A later live dispatcher could be added as a separate opt-in integration after probes and confirmation.

claim: Target support claims must stay asymmetric: Codex gets reference guidance, Claude gets symlink/native-command-friendly guidance, Copilot gets personal adapter support without repo-level .github writes, and DeepSeek support is API plus CodeWhale-harness caveated.
gaps: Live provider behavior was not probed in this research run.

claim: Implementation should proceed in phases: attribution/spec, non-runtime canonical skill, optional separate runtime companion, manifest/profile wiring, target adapters/docs/tests, then optional dry-run delegation probes.
gaps: Detailed code-level task breakdown requires a follow-up implementation phase.

claim: To work for all install targets, the plan must include Codex, Claude, DeepSeek, Copilot, and OpenClaw, but OpenClaw must remain explicit fake-root-only with runtime-backed skills blocked until native target evidence exists.
gaps: Real-system OpenClaw writes and native loader behavior remain intentionally unverified and blocked.

claim: To work across all OS/substrates, the implementation must provide POSIX and native Windows runtime entrypoints and verify Linux, macOS, Windows, WSL, and mounted-profile path shapes separately; Linux-hosted Windows-shape checks are not proof of native Windows execution.
gaps: Native Windows and macOS commands were not run in this session.

claim: The acceptance plan should require lifecycle-test platform-shape all, fake-root lifecycle for the new skill/profile, explicit OpenClaw fake-root coverage, runtime-smoke for the companion only after an offline smoke contract exists, docs checks, tests, and native Windows make.bat checks before claiming all-target/all-OS readiness.
gaps: This is a verification plan; it has not yet been executed for the new skill because the skill is not implemented.

claim: The canonical autonomous-research-loop skill must remain non-runtime-backed and support-file-free if it is to be installable in explicit OpenClaw fake roots under current planner rules; runtime helpers must be a separate companion skill or otherwise excluded from OpenClaw until neutral runtime evidence exists.
gaps: Future OpenClaw native target evidence could change this, but current implementation blocks runtime-backed OpenClaw skill files.

claim: Runtime smoke should be required only after the companion runtime helper has a manifest-declared offline smoke contract; until then, lifecycle and fake-root checks are necessary but not sufficient for runtime readiness.
gaps: The planned autonomous runtime helper does not exist yet.

claim: OpenClaw coverage must be tested through explicit OpenClaw target selection because OpenClaw is not default-detected and fake-root lifecycle commands without an OpenClaw agent filter do not prove OpenClaw behavior.
gaps: The exact implementation command can be direct CLI with global --agents openclaw or a dedicated test if make wrappers do not expose global agent arguments.
